{{infobox football tournament season
|title                      = Copa do Brasil
|year                       = 2019
|other_titles               =
|image                      = File:2019_Copa_do_Brasil_logo.jpg
|imagesize                  = 170 px
|caption                    = 
|country                    = Brazil
|dates                      = 5 February – 18 September
|num_teams                  = 91
|champions                  = [[Club Athletico Paranaense|Athletico Paranaense]] {{small|(1st title)}}
|runner-up                  = [[Sport Club Internacional|Internacional]]
|continentalcup1            = [[2020 Copa Libertadores]]
|continentalcup1 qualifiers = [[Club Athletico Paranaense|Athletico Paranaense]]
|matches                    = 120
|goals                      = 270
|top goal scorer            = [[Paolo Guerrero]]<br>[[Luciano da Rocha Neves|Luciano]]<br>[[Pipico]]<br>(5 goals each)
|player                     = {{nowrap|[[Paolo Guerrero]] (Internacional)}}
|prevseason                 = [[2018 Copa do Brasil|2018]]
|nextseason                 = [[2020 Copa do Brasil|2020]]
}}

The '''2019 Copa do Brasil''' (officially the '''Copa [[Continental AG|Continental Pneus]] do Brasil 2019''' for sponsorship reasons)<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.meioemensagem.com.br/home/ultimas-noticias/2018/12/18/continental-renova-naming-rights-da-copa-do-brasil.html |title=Continental renova naming rights da Copa do Brasil |date=18 December 2018 |publisher=Meio & Mensagem |language=Portuguese}}</ref> was the 31st edition of the [[Copa do Brasil]] football competition. It was held between 5 February and 18 September 2019.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.cbf.com.br/a-cbf/informes/index/cbf-divulga-calendario-do-futebol-brasileiro-para-2019 |title=CBF divulga calendário do futebol brasileiro para 2019 |date=3 October 2018 |publisher=CBF |language=Portuguese}}</ref> The competition was contested by 91 teams, either qualified through participating in their respective state championships (70), by the 2019 [[CBF ranking]] (10), by the [[2018 Copa do Nordeste]] (1), by the [[2018 Copa Verde]] (1), by the [[2018 Campeonato Brasileiro Série B|2018 Série B]] (1) or those qualified for [[2019 Copa Libertadores]] (8).

[[Club Athletico Paranaense|Athletico Paranaense]] defeated [[Sport Club Internacional|Internacional]] 3–1 on aggregate in the finals to win their first title.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.cbf.com.br/futebol-brasileiro/competicoes/copa-brasil-masculino/2019/120 |title=Athletico-PR bate o Inter e conquista título inédito da Copa do Brasil |date=18 September 2019 |publisher=CBF |language=Portuguese}}</ref> As champions, [[Club Athletico Paranaense|Athletico Paranaense]] qualified for the [[2020 Supercopa do Brasil]], the [[2020 Copa Libertadores]] group stage and the [[2020 Copa do Brasil]] Round of 16.<ref name="regulations">{{cite web|url=https://conteudo.cbf.com.br/cdn/201812/20181211183733_557.pdf |title=Regulamento Específico da Competição Copa do Brasil 2019 |publisher=CBF |language=Portuguese}}</ref>

[[Cruzeiro Esporte Clube|Cruzeiro]] were the defending champions, but lost in the semifinals 0–4 on aggregate to [[Sport Club Internacional|Internacional]].

[[Paolo Guerrero]] (Internacional) and [[Fábio (footballer, born 1980)|Fábio]] (Cruzeiro) won best player and best goalkeeper awards, respectively.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://globoesporte.globo.com/rs/futebol/times/internacional/noticia/um-mes-apos-vice-guerrero-recebe-premio-de-artilheiro-e-craque-da-copa-do-brasil.ghtml |title=Um mês após vice, Guerrero recebe prêmio de artilheiro e craque da Copa do Brasil |publisher=Globo |language=Portuguese |date=22 October 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.torcedores.com/noticias/2019/09/goleiro-fabio-do-cruzeiro-conquista-luva-de-ouro-da-copa-do-brasil |title=Goleiro Fábio, do Cruzeiro, conquista "Luva de Ouro" da Copa do Brasil |publisher=Torcedores |language=Portuguese |date=19 September 2018}}</ref>

==Format==
The competition was a [[single elimination]] [[knockout tournament]], the first two stages featuring a single match and the other stages featuring [[two-legged tie]]s. Eleven teams qualified for the Round of 16 (Teams qualified for 2019 Copa Libertadores (8), Série B champions, Copa Verde champions and Copa do Nordeste champions). The remaining 80 teams played the first stage. The 40 winners played the second stage, the 20 winners played the third stage, the 10 winners played the fourth stage. Finally, the five fourth-stage winners qualified for the Round of 16.<ref name="regulations"/>

==Qualified teams==
Teams in '''bold''' are qualified directly for the round of 16.
{| class="wikitable"
|-
!width=130|Association
!width=210|Team
!Qualification method
|-
|rowspan=2 |{{flagicon|Acre}} [[Federação de Futebol do Acre|Acre]]<br>{{small|2 berths}}
|[[Rio Branco Football Club|Rio Branco]]
|2018 [[Campeonato Acriano]] champions
|-
|[[Galvez Esporte Clube|Galvez]]
|2018 [[Campeonato Acriano]] runners-up
|-
|rowspan=3 |{{flagicon|Alagoas}} [[Federação Alagoana de Futebol|Alagoas]]<br>{{small|3 berths}}
|[[Centro Sportivo Alagoano|CSA]]
|2018 [[Campeonato Alagoano]] champions
|-
|[[Clube de Regatas Brasil|CRB]]
|2018 [[Campeonato Alagoano]] runners-up
|-
|[[Agremiação Sportiva Arapiraquense|ASA]]
|2018 [[Campeonato Alagoano]] 3rd place
|-
|{{flagicon|Amapá}} [[Federação Amapaense de Futebol|Amapá]]<br>{{small|1 berth}}
|[[Ypiranga Clube|Ypiranga]]
|2018 [[Campeonato Amapaense]] champions
|-
|rowspan=2 |{{flagicon|Amazonas}} [[Federação Amazonense de Futebol|Amazonas]]<br>{{small|2 berths}}
|[[Manaus Futebol Clube|Manaus]]
|2018 [[Campeonato Amazonense]] champions
|-
|[[Nacional Fast Clube|Fast Clube]]
|2018 [[Campeonato Amazonense]] runners-up
|-
|rowspan=3 |{{flagicon|Bahia}} [[Federação Bahiana de Futebol|Bahia]]<br>{{small|3 berths}}
|[[Esporte Clube Bahia|Bahia]]
|2018 [[Campeonato Baiano]] champions
|-
|[[Esporte Clube Vitória|Vitória]]
|2018 [[Campeonato Baiano]] runners-up
|-
|[[Sociedade Desportiva Juazeirense|Juazeirense]]
|2018 [[Campeonato Baiano]] 3rd place
|-
|rowspan=4 |{{flagicon|Ceará}} [[Federação Cearense de Futebol|Ceará]]<br>{{small|3 + 1 berths}}
|'''[[Fortaleza Esporte Clube|Fortaleza]]'''
|'''[[2018 Campeonato Brasileiro Série B]] champions'''
|-
|[[Ceará Sporting Club|Ceará]]
|2018 [[Campeonato Cearense]] champions
|-
|[[FC Atlético Cearense|Atlético Cearense]]{{ref label|id4|a|a}}
|2018 [[Campeonato Cearense]] 3rd place
|-
|[[Ferroviário Atlético Clube (CE)|Ferroviário]]
|2018 [[Copa Fares Lopes]] champions
|-
|{{flagicon|Espírito Santo}} [[Federação de Futebol do Estado do Espírito Santo|Espírito Santo]]<br>{{small|1 berth}}
|[[Sociedade Desportiva Serra Futebol Clube|Serra]]
|2018 [[Campeonato Capixaba]] champions
|-
|rowspan=2 |{{flagicon|Distrito Federal}} [[Federação de Futebol do Distrito Federal|Federal District]]<br>{{small|2 berths}}
|[[Sobradinho Esporte Clube|Sobradinho]]
|2018 [[Campeonato Brasiliense]] champions
|-
|[[Brasiliense F.C.|Brasiliense]]
|2018 [[Campeonato Brasiliense]] runners-up
|-
|rowspan=4 |{{flagicon|Goiás}} [[Federação Goiana de Futebol|Goiás]]<br>{{small|3 + 1 berths}}
|[[Goiás Esporte Clube|Goiás]]
|2018 [[Campeonato Goiano]] champions
|-
|[[Associação Atlética Aparecidense|Aparecidense]]
|2018 [[Campeonato Goiano]] runners-up
|-
|[[Vila Nova Futebol Clube|Vila Nova]]
|2018 [[Campeonato Goiano]] 3rd place
|-
|[[Atlético Clube Goianiense|Atlético Goianiense]]
|2nd best placed team in the 2018 [[CBF ranking]] not already qualified
|-
|rowspan=3 |{{flagicon|Maranhão}} [[Federação Maranhense de Futebol|Maranhão]]<br>{{small|2 + 1 berths}}
|'''[[Sampaio Corrêa Futebol Clube|Sampaio Corrêa]]'''
|'''[[2018 Copa do Nordeste]] champions'''
|-
|[[Moto Club de São Luís|Moto Club]]
|2018 [[Campeonato Maranhense]] champions
|-
|[[Sociedade Imperatriz de Desportos|Imperatriz]]
|2018 [[Campeonato Maranhense]] runners-up 
|-
|rowspan=4 |{{flagicon|Mato Grosso}} [[Federação Matogrossense de Futebol|Mato Grosso]]<br>{{small|3 + 1 berths}}
|[[Cuiabá Esporte Clube|Cuiabá]]
|2018 [[Campeonato Mato-Grossense]] champions
|-
|[[Sinop Futebol Clube|Sinop]]
|2018 [[Campeonato Mato-Grossense]] runners-up
|-
|[[Mixto Esporte Clube|Mixto]]
|2018 [[Copa FMF]] champions
|-
|[[Luverdense Esporte Clube|Luverdense]]
|5th best placed team in the 2018 [[CBF ranking]] not already qualified
|-
|rowspan=2 |{{flagicon|Mato Grosso do Sul}} [[Federação de Futebol de Mato Grosso do Sul|Mato Grosso do Sul]] {{small|2 berths}}
|[[Operário Futebol Clube (MS)|Operário]]
|2018 [[Campeonato Sul-Mato-Grossense]] champions
|-
|[[Corumbaense Futebol Clube|Corumbaense]]
|2018 [[Campeonato Sul-Mato-Grossense]] runners-up
|-
|rowspan=7 |{{flagicon|Minas Gerais}} [[Federação Mineira de Futebol|Minas Gerais]]<br>{{small|4 + 2 + 1 berths}}
|'''[[Cruzeiro Esporte Clube|Cruzeiro]]'''
|'''[[2018 Copa do Brasil]] champions'''
|-
|'''[[Clube Atlético Mineiro|Atlético Mineiro]]'''
|'''[[2018 Campeonato Brasileiro Série A]] 6th place'''
|-
|[[América Futebol Clube (MG)|América Mineiro]]
|[[2018 Campeonato Mineiro]] 3rd place
|-
|[[Tupi Football Club|Tupi]]
|[[2018 Campeonato Mineiro]] 4th place
|-
|[[Tombense Futebol Clube|Tombense]]
|[[2018 Campeonato Mineiro]] 5th place
|-
|[[União Recreativa dos Trabalhadores|URT]]
|[[2018 Campeonato Mineiro]] 6th place
|-
|[[Boa Esporte Clube|Boa Esporte]]
|10th best placed team in the 2018 [[CBF ranking]] not already qualified
|-
|rowspan=4 |{{flagicon|Pará}} [[Federação Paraense de Futebol|Pará]]<br>{{small|3 + 1 berths}}
|'''[[Paysandu Sport Club|Paysandu]]'''
|'''[[2018 Copa Verde]] champions'''
|-
|[[Clube do Remo|Remo]]
|[[2018 Campeonato Paraense]] champions
|-
|[[Bragantino Clube do Pará|Bragantino]]
|[[2018 Campeonato Paraense]] 3rd place
|-
|[[São Raimundo Esporte Clube (PA)|São Raimundo]]
|[[2018 Campeonato Paraense]] 4th place
|-
|rowspan=2 |{{flagicon|Paraíba}} [[Federação Paraibana de Futebol|Paraíba]]<br>{{small|2 berths}}
|[[Botafogo Futebol Clube (PB)|Botafogo]]
|[[2018 Campeonato Paraibano]] champions
|-
|[[Campinense Clube|Campinense]]
|[[2018 Campeonato Paraibano]] runners-up
|-
|rowspan=5 |{{flagicon|Paraná}} [[Federação Paranaense de Futebol|Paraná]]<br>{{small|3 + 1 + 1 berths}}
|'''[[Club Athletico Paranaense|Athletico Paranaense]]'''
|'''[[2018 Copa Sudamericana]] champions'''
|-
|[[Coritiba Foot Ball Club|Coritiba]]
|2018 [[Campeonato Paranaense]] runners-up
|-
|[[Foz do Iguaçu Futebol Clube|Foz do Iguaçu]]
|2018 [[Campeonato Paranaense]] 3rd place
|-
|[[Paraná Clube|Paraná]]
|2018 [[Campeonato Paranaense]] 4th place
|-
|[[Londrina Esporte Clube|Londrina]]
|7th best placed team in the 2018 [[CBF ranking]] not already qualified
|-
|rowspan=4 |{{flagicon|Pernambuco}} [[Federação Pernambucana de Futebol|Pernambuco]]<br>{{small|3 + 1 berths}}
|[[Clube Náutico Capibaribe|Náutico]]
|[[2018 Campeonato Pernambucano]] champions
|-
|[[Central Sport Club|Central]]
|[[2018 Campeonato Pernambucano]] runners-up
|-
|[[Sport Club do Recife|Sport]]
|[[2018 Campeonato Pernambucano]] 3rd place
|-
|[[Santa Cruz Futebol Clube|Santa Cruz]]
|3rd best placed team in the 2018 [[CBF ranking]] not already qualified
|-
|rowspan=2 |{{flagicon|Piauí}} [[Federação de Futebol do Piauí|Piauí]]<br>{{small|2 berths}}
|[[Associação Atlética de Altos|Altos]]
|2018 [[Campeonato Piauiense]] champions
|-
|[[Ríver Atlético Clube|Ríver]]
|2018 [[Campeonato Piauiense]] runners-up
|-
|rowspan=6 |{{flagicon|Rio de Janeiro}} [[Federação de Futebol do Estado do Rio de Janeiro|Rio de Janeiro]]<br>{{small|5 + 1 berths}}
|'''[[Clube de Regatas do Flamengo|Flamengo]]'''
|'''[[2018 Campeonato Brasileiro Série A]] runners-up'''
|-
|[[Botafogo de Futebol e Regatas|Botafogo]]
|[[2018 Campeonato Carioca]] champions
|-
|[[CR Vasco da Gama|Vasco da Gama]]
|[[2018 Campeonato Carioca]] runners-up
|-
|[[Fluminense FC|Fluminense]]
|[[2018 Campeonato Carioca]] 4th place
|-
|[[Boavista Sport Club|Boavista]]
|[[2018 Campeonato Carioca]] 5th place
|-
|[[Americano Futebol Clube|Americano]]
|2018 [[Copa Rio (state cup)|Copa Rio]] champions
|-
|rowspan=3 |{{nowrap|{{flagicon|Rio Grande do Norte}} [[Federação Norte-Riograndense de Futebol|Rio Grande do Norte]]}} {{small|3 berths}}
|[[ABC Futebol Clube|ABC]]
|2018 [[Campeonato Potiguar]] champions
|-
|[[América Futebol Clube (RN)|América de Natal]]
|2018 [[Campeonato Potiguar]] runners-up
|-
|[[Santa Cruz Futebol Clube (RN)|Santa Cruz de Natal]]
|2018 [[Campeonato Potiguar]] 3rd place
|-
|rowspan=7 |{{flagicon|Rio Grande do Sul}} [[Federação Gaúcha de Futebol|Rio Grande do Sul]]<br>{{small|4 + 2 + 1 berths}}
|'''[[Sport Club Internacional|Internacional]]'''
|'''[[2018 Campeonato Brasileiro Série A]] 3rd place'''
|-
|'''[[Grêmio Foot-Ball Porto Alegrense|Grêmio]]'''
|'''[[2018 Campeonato Brasileiro Série A]] 4th place'''
|-
|[[Grêmio Esportivo Brasil|Brasil de Pelotas]]
|[[2018 Campeonato Gaúcho]] runners-up
|-
|[[Esporte Clube São José|São José]]
|[[2018 Campeonato Gaúcho]] 3rd place
|-
|[[Esporte Clube Avenida|Avenida]]
|[[2018 Campeonato Gaúcho]] 4th place
|-
|[[Ypiranga Futebol Clube|Ypiranga]]
|2018 [[Copa FGF]] 3rd place
|-
|[[Esporte Clube Juventude|Juventude]]
|6th best placed team in the 2018 [[CBF ranking]] not already qualified
|-
|{{flagicon|Rondônia}} [[Federação de Futebol do Estado de Rondônia|Rondônia]]<br>{{small|1 berth}}
|[[Real Desportivo Ariquemes Futebol Clube|Real Ariquemes]]
|2018 [[Campeonato Rondoniense]] champions
|-
|{{flagicon|Roraima}} [[Federação Roraimense de Futebol|Roraima]]<br>{{small|1 berth}}
|[[São Raimundo Esporte Clube (RR)|São Raimundo]]
|2018 [[Campeonato Roraimense]] champions
|-
|rowspan=7 |{{flagicon|Santa Catarina}} [[Federação Catarinense de Futebol|Santa Catarina]]<br>{{small|4 + 3 berths}}
|[[Figueirense FC|Figueirense]]
|2018 [[Campeonato Catarinense]] champions
|-
|[[Associação Chapecoense de Futebol|Chapecoense]]
|2018 [[Campeonato Catarinense]] runners-up
|-
|[[Clube Atlético Tubarão|Tubarão]]
|2018 [[Campeonato Catarinense]] 3rd place
|-
|[[Brusque Futebol Clube|Brusque]]
|2018 [[Copa Santa Catarina]] champions
|-
|[[Avaí FC|Avaí]]
|best placed team in the 2018 [[CBF ranking]] not already qualified
|-
|[[Criciúma Esporte Clube|Criciúma]]
|4th best placed team in the 2018 [[CBF ranking]] not already qualified
|-
|[[Joinville Esporte Clube|Joinville]]
|9th best placed team in the 2018 [[CBF ranking]] not already qualified
|-
|rowspan=8 |{{flagicon|São Paulo}} [[Federação Paulista de Futebol|São Paulo]]<br>{{small|5 + 2 + 1 berths}}
|'''[[Sociedade Esportiva Palmeiras|Palmeiras]]'''
|'''[[2018 Campeonato Brasileiro Série A]] champions'''
|-
|'''[[São Paulo FC|São Paulo]]'''
|'''[[2018 Campeonato Brasileiro Série A]] 5th place'''
|-
|[[Sport Club Corinthians Paulista|Corinthians]]
|[[2018 Campeonato Paulista]] champions
|-
|[[Santos FC|Santos]]
|[[2018 Campeonato Paulista]] 4th place
|-
|[[Associação Atlética Ponte Preta|Ponte Preta]]
|2018 [[Campeonato Paulista#Campeonato Paulista do Interior|Campeonato Paulista do Interior]] champions
|-
|[[Guarani FC|Guarani]]
|2018 [[Campeonato Paulista Série A2]] champions
|-
|[[Clube Atlético Votuporanguense|Votuporanguense]]
|[[2018 Copa Paulista]] champions
|-
|[[Oeste Futebol Clube|Oeste]]
|8th best placed team in the 2018 [[CBF ranking]] not already qualified
|-
|rowspan=2 |{{flagicon|Sergipe}} [[Federação Sergipana de Futebol|Sergipe]]<br>{{small|2 berths}}
|[[Club Sportivo Sergipe|Sergipe]]
|2018 [[Campeonato Sergipano]] champions
|-
|[[Associação Olímpica de Itabaiana|Itabaiana]]
|2018 [[Campeonato Sergipano]] runners-up
|-
|{{flagicon|Tocantins}} [[Federação Tocantinense de Futebol|Tocantins]]<br>{{small|1 berth}}
|[[Palmas Futebol e Regatas|Palmas]]
|2018 [[Campeonato Tocantinense]] champions
|}
:{{note label|id4||a}}{{small|Uniclinic Atlético Clube was renamed to Futebol Clube Atlético Cearense on 21 September 2018.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.futebolcearense.com.br/2011/noticia_ver.asp?id=9402 |title=Uniclinic agora é Futebol Clube Atlético Cearense |publisher=Federação Cearense de Futebol |language=Portuguese |date=21 September 2018}}</ref>}}

==Schedule==
The schedule of the competition was as follows.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://conteudo.cbf.com.br/cdn/201812/20181210192827_498.pdf |title=COPA DO BRASIL - TABELA BÁSICA / EDIÇÃO 2019 |publisher=CBF |language=Portuguese}}</ref>

{| class="wikitable"
|-
!Stage
!First leg
!Second leg
|-
|First stage
|colspan=2|{{unbulleted list
|Week 1: 6 February 2019
|Week 2: 13 February 2019
|Replay: 3 April 2019
}}
|-
|Second stage
|colspan=2|{{unbulleted list
|Week 1: 20 February 2019
|Week 2: 27 February 2019
|Week 3: 6 March 2019
|Postponed: 10 April 2019
}}
|-
|Third stage
|Week 1: 13 March 2019<br>Week 2: 27 March 2019<br>Week 3: 3 April 2019<br>Week 4: 16 April 2019
|Week 1: 3 April 2019<br>Week 2: 10 April 2019<br>Week 3: 20 April 2019
|-
|Fourth stage
|17 April 2019
|24 April 2019
|-
|Round of 16
|15, 22 and 29 May 2019
|22 and 29 May and 5 June 2019
|-
|Quarterfinals
|10 July 2019
|17 July 2019
|-
|Semifinals
|7 and 14 August 2019
|4 September 2019
|-
|Finals
|11 September 2019
|18 September 2019
|}

==Draw==
{{#lst:2019 Copa do Brasil First Stage|Draw}}
{{clear}}

==First stage==
{{main|2019 Copa do Brasil First Stage#First Stage|l1=2019 Copa do Brasil first stage}}
{{#lst:2019 Copa do Brasil First Stage|Format}}
{{#lst:2019 Copa do Brasil First Stage|First stage}}

==Second stage==
{{main|2019 Copa do Brasil Second Stage#Second Stage|l1=2019 Copa do Brasil second stage}}
{{#lst:2019 Copa do Brasil Second Stage|Format}}
{{#lst:2019 Copa do Brasil Second Stage|Second stage}}

==Third stage==
{{main|2019 Copa do Brasil Third Stage#Third Stage|l1=2019 Copa do Brasil third stage}}
{{#lst:2019 Copa do Brasil Third Stage|Format}}
{{#lst:2019 Copa do Brasil Third Stage|Third stage}}

==Fourth stage==
{{main|2019 Copa do Brasil Fourth Stage#Fourth Stage|l1=2019 Copa do Brasil fourth stage}}
{{#lst:2019 Copa do Brasil Fourth Stage|Draw2}}
{{#lst:2019 Copa do Brasil Fourth Stage|Format}}
{{#lst:2019 Copa do Brasil Fourth Stage|Fourth stage}}

==Final stages==
{{main|2019 Copa do Brasil Final Stages#Final Stages|l1=2019 Copa do Brasil final stages}}
{{#lst:2019 Copa do Brasil Final Stages|Format}}

===Bracket===
{{#lst:2019 Copa do Brasil Final Stages|Bracket}}

===Round of 16===
{{#lst:2019 Copa do Brasil Final Stages|Draw3}}
{{#lst:2019 Copa do Brasil Final Stages|Round of 16}}

===Quarter-finals===
{{#lst:2019 Copa do Brasil Final Stages|Draw4}}
{{#lst:2019 Copa do Brasil Final Stages|Quarterfinals}}

===Semi-finals===
{{#lst:2019 Copa do Brasil Final Stages|Draw5}}
{{#lst:2019 Copa do Brasil Final Stages|Semifinals}}

===Finals===
{{main|2019 Copa do Brasil Finals}}
{{#lst:2019 Copa do Brasil Final Stages|Draw6}}
{{#lst:2019 Copa do Brasil Final Stages|Matches}}
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center; margin: 0 auto;"
|-
!2019 Copa do Brasil Champions
|-
|'''[[Club Athletico Paranaense|Athletico Paranaense]]'''<br>'''1st Title'''
|}

==Top goalscorers==
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; font-size:90%"
!Rank
!Player
!Team
!{{Tooltip|1S|First stage}}
!{{Tooltip|2S|Second stage}}
!{{Tooltip|3S1|Third stage 1st leg}}
!{{Tooltip|3S2|Third stage 2nd leg}}
!{{Tooltip|4S1|Fourth stage 1st leg}}
!{{Tooltip|4S2|Fourth stage 2nd leg}}
!{{Tooltip|⅛F1|Round of 16 ("Eighth-finals") 1st leg}}
!{{Tooltip|⅛F2|Round of 16 ("Eighth-finals") 2nd leg}}
!{{Tooltip|QF1|Quarter-finals 1st leg}}
!{{Tooltip|QF2|Quarter-finals 2nd leg}}
!{{Tooltip|SF1|Semi-finals 1st leg}}
!{{Tooltip|SF2|Semi-finals 2nd leg}}
!{{Tooltip|F1|Final 1st leg}}
!{{Tooltip|F2|Final 2nd leg}}
!Total
|-
!rowspan=3|1
|align=left|{{flagicon|PER}} [[Paolo Guerrero]]
|align=left|{{flagicon|Rio Grande do Sul}} [[Sport Club Internacional|Internacional]]
|bgcolor="silver" colspan=6| ||2||1||0||0||0||2||0||0|0
!rowspan=3|5
|-
|align=left|{{flagicon|BRA}} [[Luciano da Rocha Neves|Luciano]]
|align=left|{{flagicon|Rio de Janeiro}} [[Fluminense FC|Fluminense]]
|2||1||0||1||1||0||0||0||bgcolor="silver" colspan=6 |
|-
|align=left|{{flagicon|BRA}} [[Pipico]]
|align=left|{{flagicon|Pernambuco}} [[Santa Cruz Futebol Clube|Santa Cruz]]
|1||1||0||2||0||1||bgcolor="silver" colspan=8 |
|-
!rowspan=4|4
|align=left|{{flagicon|BRA}} [[Daniel Amorim Dias da Silva|Daniel Amorim]]
|align=left|{{flagicon|Santa Catarina}} [[Avaí FC|Avaí]]
|2||2||0||0||bgcolor="silver" colspan=10 |
!rowspan=4|4
|-
|align=left|{{flagicon|BRA}} [[Gilberto Oliveira Souza Júnior|Gilberto]]
|align=left|{{flagicon|Bahia}} [[Esporte Clube Bahia|Bahia]]
|2||1||0||0||0||0||x||0||1||0||bgcolor="silver" colspan=4 |
|-
|align=left|{{flagicon|BRA}} [[Luis Fernando Batista|Nando]]
|align=left|{{flagicon|Paraíba}} [[Botafogo Futebol Clube (PB)|Botafogo]]
|2||0||0||2||bgcolor="silver" colspan=10 |
|-
|align=left|{{flagicon|URU}} [[Carlos Andrés Sánchez|Carlos Sánchez]]
|align=left|{{flagicon|São Paulo}} [[Santos FC|Santos]]
|2||0||x||2||0||0||x||0||bgcolor="silver" colspan=6 |
|-
!rowspan=7|8
|align=left|{{flagicon|BRA}} [[Clayton da Rosa Peixoto|Clayton]]
|align=left|{{flagicon|Paraíba}} [[Botafogo Futebol Clube (PB)|Botafogo]]
|0||2||0||1||bgcolor="silver" colspan=10 |
!rowspan=7|3
|-
|align=left|{{flagicon|BRA}} [[Erik Lima|Erik]]
|align=left|{{flagicon|Rio de Janeiro}} [[Botafogo de Futebol e Regatas|Botafogo]]
|0||2||1||0||bgcolor="silver" colspan=10 |
|-
|align=left|{{flagicon|BRA}} [[Gustavo Henrique da Silva Sousa|Gustavo]]
|align=left|{{flagicon|São Paulo}} [[Sport Club Corinthians Paulista|Corinthians]]
|2||1||x||0||x||x||x||0||bgcolor="silver" colspan=6 |
|-
|align=left|{{flagicon|BRA}} [[Jenison de Jesus Brito e Brito|Jenison]]
|align=left|{{flagicon|Paraná}} [[Paraná Clube|Paraná]]
|2||1||bgcolor="silver" colspan=12 |
|-
|align=left|{{flagicon|BRA}} [[Marco Vinícius Faleiro de Siqueira|Marco Goiano]]
|align=left|{{flagicon|Pará}} [[Bragantino Clube do Pará|Bragantino]]
|0||2||0||1||bgcolor="silver" colspan=10 |
|-
|align=left|{{flagicon|BRA}} [[Rodrygo Goes|Rodrygo]]
|align=left|{{flagicon|São Paulo}} [[Santos FC|Santos]]
|x||1||0||1||1||0||0||x||bgcolor="silver" colspan=6 |
|-
|align=left|{{flagicon|BRA}} [[Thiago Neves]]
|align=left|{{flagicon|Minas Gerais}} [[Cruzeiro Esporte Clube|Cruzeiro]]
|bgcolor="silver" colspan=6| ||x||2||1||x||0||0||bgcolor="silver" colspan=2 |
|}
{{small|Source:CBF<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.cbf.com.br/futebol-brasileiro/competicoes/copa-brasil-masculino |title=Artilharia |publisher=CBF |language=Portuguese}}</ref>}}

==References==
{{Reflist}}

{{Copa do Brasil}}
{{2019 in Brazilian football}}
{{2019 in South American football (CONMEBOL)}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:Copa do Brasil 2019}}
[[Category:2019 Copa do Brasil| ]]
[[Category:Copa do Brasil seasons|2019]]
[[Category:2019 in Brazilian football]]
[[Category:2019 in domestic association football cups]]
